title: feat: bill Vertex explicit-cache creates and storage via the adjustments system
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `promptCacheWriteTokens` (= standard input rate) to the 5 Vertex gemini chat models — the creating request reports its written prefix as `cache_creation_input_tokens` (gateway#8) which already maps to this price via `usage-headers.ts`; Tinybird columns existed all along
- Adds a `google.vertex.cache_storage.v1` billing adjustment ($1.00/1M token-hours flash, $4.50/1M pro — verified against GCP Billing Catalog SKUs; 1h TTL per create), counted from `usage.cache_creation_input_tokens` in the response output
- Regression tests: gen ingress preserves content-block `cache_control` markers (drops message-level, documented), gemini transform pipes don't strip markers, cache-write pricing + storage adjustment unit tests
- Regenerates the effective-prices snapshot (includes #12210's registry drift — that PR's branch predates the snapshot)

**Stacked on #12210** (`claude/billing-map` merged in) — diff shrinks to the caching changes once that merges.

**Verified on staging** (staging.gen.myceli.ai from this branch → rubeus-dev gateway from pollinations/gateway#8 → real Vertex): create → `cached=0, cache_creation=7931`, match + stream match → `cached=7931`; staging Tinybird rows show `token_count_prompt_cache_write=7931`, `adjustment_costs={google.vertex.cache_storage.v1: 0.007931}`, hit price 94× cheaper than create.

**Deploy order (billing-safe):** this PR merges + deploys to gen production BEFORE the `PORTKEY_COMMIT` bump to gateway#8 — the registry price is inert until the gateway emits the field; the reverse order would bill created prefixes at $0.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Memory is cheap
gist.blurb: Vertex Gemini prompt caching is now live. Stash those massive system prompts in memory and watch your token costs plummet on repeated requests.
gist.summary: Added explicit prompt caching support for 5 Vertex Gemini chat models, tracking cache creation and storage costs ($1.00/1M token-hours for Flash, $4.50/1M for Pro).
gist.impact: Developers can now use cache_control markers with Gemini models to cache large prefixes, dropping the cost of repeated hits by up to 94x.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing glowing memory blocks (representing cache) into a retro server rack, with the Monitor Robot looking excited at the cost savings.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, organizing glowing lime green (#ecf874) memory blocks into a chunky retro server rack. The Monitor Robot stands nearby, its screen displaying a happy face and a downward trending chart. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm and nostalgic.
